metadata.title: The Question Nobody Was Asking
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The author asks whether the project truly proved that personalized learning works. Three children received personalized AI tutoring for fifteen dollars total. The build cost five dollars in API credits and five hours of work. Low cost is demonstrated, but a three-child case cannot prove learning effectiveness.

artifactLines: The author asks whether the project truly proved that personalized learning works.
Three children received personalized AI tutoring for fifteen dollars total.
The build cost five dollars in API credits and five hours of work.
Low cost is demonstrated, but a three-child case cannot prove learning effectiveness.

beats.narration_text: The honest question of what was actually proven, when three children got personalized tutoring for fifteen dollars total, five in API credits and five hours of work, makes me want to separate anecdote from evidence. Help me: (1) state what a three-child, no-control demonstration can and cannot prove about personalized learning; (2) design the smallest next study that would add real evidence; (3) explain why the fifteen-dollar cost is the strongest claim here, not the learning outcome.

props.command: The honest question of what was actually proven, when three children got personalized tutoring for fifteen dollars total, five in API credits and five hours of work, makes me want to separate anecdote from evidence. Help me: (1) state what a three-child, no-control demonstration can and cannot prove about personalized learning; (2) design the smallest next study that would add real evidence; (3) explain why the fifteen-dollar cost is the strongest claim here, not the learning outcome.
output: An Investigation into Production Collapse and the Questions We Sh
Did I prove that personalized learning works
Before you answer, let me tell you what happened

beats.narration_text: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — building real projects and shipping real research.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Written by Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. Humanitarians AI is an educational bridge program where Fellows learn experiential AI by doing AI — build

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude — Take the central idea from 'The Question Nobody Was Asking' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each.… That's the prompt that actually tests whether you got it. Run it and see where it surprises you.

props.command: Take the central idea from 'The Question Nobody Was Asking' and give me the three most actionable takeaways — one sentence each. Then name the failure mode I'm most likely to hit.
